name: drag_coalesce_3frames
comment: Three same-name same-target move_selection frames (dx 5,7,3) coalesce into ONE txn whose move op is dx:15. The leading select_rect establishes (and pins) the byte-equal targets.

name: drag_coalesce_3frames_by_ids
comment: Id-primary twin: three same-name same-id move_by_ids frames (dx 4,6,2) coalesce into ONE txn whose move op is dx:12. Each move_by_ids re-selects from its own ids before translating.

name: drag_coalesce_breaks_different_name
comment: A DIFFERENT transaction name between two same-target moves breaks the run: two deliberate separate gestures stay distinct undo steps.

name: drag_coalesce_breaks_different_target
comment: An intervening select to a DIFFERENT element re-selects per frame, so each frame is a 2-op txn (select+move) on a different target set; the run never coalesces. The single-op different-target break (predicate c proper) is pinned in the Rust drag_coalesce_target_break test, which changes selection out-of-band between two single-op move frames.

name: drag_coalesce_copy_absorbs_trailing_drag
comment: The alt-drag-copy gesture (selection.yaml) journals copy_selection and then drags the NEW duplicate with move_selection frames — drag-to-duplicate is ONE action, so the trailing move FOLDS into the copy's own offset (commit_txn Branch A): copy(dx:0) + move(dx:7) => copy(dx:7), ONE undo step. The PRECEDING move on the ORIGINAL (dx:5, kept — not reverted by preview.restore here) is a different op on a different target set and stays its own step. Two same-name moves separated by a copy are therefore TWO undo steps, not three: [move-original, copy-dragged]. (A copy still never coalesces with another copy, and never folds BACKWARD into a kept preceding move.)
